content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Eric Lucan 
Novato Councilmember 
26 Truman Drive 
Novato, CA 94947 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-168 
Dear Mr. Lucan: 
July 25, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding your duties as a Novato City 
Councilmember under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section I 090. 
QUESTION 
Do you have a conflict of interest with respect to the Novato Boulevard Improvements 
Project (the "Project") based on your property with 1,200 feet of the Boulevard? 
CONCLUSION 
Based on the distance from the Project and the substantial buffer between your property and 
the Project ( four to five developed city blocks), you will not have a conflict of interest in the 
decisions. 
FACTS 
The city is beginning the Novato Boulevard Improvements Project which will include the 
stretch of Novato Boulevard between Diab lo A venue and Grant A venue. Novato Boulevard is a 
major arterial through the city and the project will provide general improvements to the Boulevard 
between Diablo Avenue and Grant Avenue. You stated that the project is in the very early stages 
and most likely will not be constructed until 2020, but you anticipate several meetings regarding 
design and layout before the project is finally scoped and put out to bid. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 181 10 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-17-168 
Page No. 2 
You own real property on Center Road and use it as rental property. Center Road is south of 
Novato Boulevard and runs parallel with it. Your property is approximately 1,200 feet away from 
the Boulevard and approximately 278 feet from the intersection ofTamalpais Avenue and Center 
Road. Tamalpais A venue bisects Novato Boulevard to the north, approximate at the midpoint of the 
project portion of the Boulevard. 
You stated that you do not believe that the Project would change the character of your 
property by substantially altering traffic levels or intensity of use, parking, view, privacy, noise, air 
quality, etc. According to the Notice of Preparation of Draft Environmental Impact Report the 
Project's improvements would be limited to Novato Boulevard itself. This includes {1) widening of 
the Boulevard between Diab lo A venue and Grant A venue to provide for four travel lanes, two in 
each direction, with a raised median island and gutters; (2) installation of left-tum lanes at 
signalized intersections or left-tum pockets at median breaks would be made; (3) creation of 
continuous bike lanes and sidewalks are proposed on both sides of Novato Boulevard through the 
Project Corridor. 
We note that between your property on Center Road and the Project are four to five fully 
developed blocks, including two large health care facilities on either side ofTamalpais Avenue 
south of Novato Boulevard. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise 
using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a 
financial interest. Under Section 87103(b), an official has a financial interest in a decision within 
the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect on any interest specified in Section 87103. 
With respect to the decisions at issue, you have a financial interest in residential real 
properties under the Act. You also have an interest in your tenant as a source of income. You have 
not provided any facts regarding your tenant and we do not further analyze your interest in the 
tenant. 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
Under the Act, an effect on an interest is presumed foreseeable if the interest is explicitly 
involved in the decision, and an interest i_s explicitly involved in the decision if (among other 
specific situations not applicable here) the interest is a named party, or subject of, the decision. 
(Regulation 18701(a).) Your property is not "explicitly involved" in the decisions relating to the 
Project. Therefore, the effect of the decisions on their respective residences is reasonably 
foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or 
theoretical. (Regulation 18701 (b ). ) 
Regulation 18702.2(a) sets forth the standards for determining whether the reasonably 
foreseeable financial effect of a governmental decision on a public official's financial interest in 


File No. A-17-168 
Page No. 3 
real property is material. For real property not explicitly involved in a decision, a foreseeable effect 
is material if the decision: 
( I 0) Would change the character of the parcel of real property by 
substantially altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of 
property surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise 
levels, or air quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the 
market value of the real property parcel in which the official has a financial 
interest; 
••• 
( 12) Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and 
consideration under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision 
was of such a nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the 
market value of the official's property. 
Your property is not on Novato Boulevard but is 1,200 feet away. Between your property 
and Novato Boulevard are four to five developed city blocks. Thus, it is unlikely that the Project 
will affect the character of your property by substantially altering traffic levels or intensity of use, 
including parking, of property surrounding your real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise 
levels, or air quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the market value of the 
real property. Consequently, we conclude based on the distance and substantial buffer between your 
property and the Project, that your property will not be materially affected by the decisions and you 
will not have a conflict of interest. 2 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Jack C. Woodside 
General Counsel 
sistant General Counsel 
egal Division 
2 Note that as the Project progresses additional facts may develop that indicate that there will be impacts on 
your property or the streets surrounding your property. If this should occur, you should contact us for further advice.

decisions.
